Clearly lost the first 2 rounds against Elder, doctor stoppage against Bonfim, got hurt bad by Motta... I think Mederos is as mid as it gets, but Sadykhov with his style struggles until he finds the finish, and if he can't find it here, he could very well lose a close decision.

posts.author: Belal Sucks
posts.content:
Predictions: 12 of 13 Pending
|
Tied for 1st with 1762 others

Pitbull vs. Pico
Patricio Pitbull, DEC
This might be the final nail in the coffin for Pico fellas. He really should win this fight based on age, athleticism, and speed but I cant for the life of me pick him to win. Pitbull is so boring and so overrated in my opinion but you cannot discount the fact that the guy knows how to fight and knows how to win. Pico on the other hand; he's got skills that are impressive sure but his career overall has been a dissapointment. Just feels like this guys hype has well surpassed his ability in the cage and that doesnt bode well against anything but total cans.

Mederos vs. Padilla
Chris Padilla, DEC
This one's got a split decision vibe to it. Mederos is a good up and comer but he's relatively untested in comparison to Padilla. Padillia unfortunately though feels like one of these guys UFC is going to put like zero promotional effort into even if he keeps winning. I expect to see more of these matchups with prospects looking to cut the line against him, but in this one I feel pretty confident that he has the style to edge out a win in multiple different ways.

posts.author: A_Person_05
posts.content:
Predictions: 13 of 13 Pending
|
Tied for 1st with 1762 others

Procházka vs. Ulberg
Jiří Procházka, TKO, R4
Jiri wins via pressure, pace, and cardio. He will land a bomb at some point, Ulberg will look to evade, but Jiri does not let you off the hook. He will sprint to follow you and stay in your face.

Van vs. Taira
Joshua Van, DEC
Van keeps it on the feet enough, lands combination after combination at will, because he is a significantly better striker, he is light years faster, he has way better cardio. And Van will be ripping the body early and often, which will just mess Taira up. Van already fought the Japanese style grappler in Rei Tsuruya and stuffed everything.

Reyes vs. Walker
Johnny Walker, TKO, R1
0 chin vs 0 chin. But Reyes got exposed in his last fight IMO. He showed a complete inability to fight while moving forward, I think Reyes will try and be aggressive at some point in the first round and get bombed put cold.

Murzakanov vs. Costa
Paulo Costa, DEC
Call me crazy. Costa is unfinishable. The height gap this fight will be stupid. And unlike Rakic Costa will actually use his range. I think Azamat will be lunging in and hitting nothing. I think Costa just picks him apart, eats whatever is coming back at him.

Blaydes vs. Hokit
Curtis Blaydes, DEC
Too much too soon for Hokit. Blaydes is still elite.

Suarez vs. Godinez
Tatiana Suarez, DEC
Suarez boring decision DC glazes her on commentary.

Swanson vs. Landwehr
Cub Swanson, DEC
BANGER. I like Cub here. Cub protects himself well, has seen it all out there. I think this will be an absolute scrap though.

Holland vs. Brown
Randy Brown, DEC
Brown will basically be landing at will against Holland from an insane range. The question is whether Holland is gonna sneak in a shot that drops or hurts Brown, and then takes it to the ground. Because if Holland hurts Brown, or gets him down I think he can finish him. But I’m inclined to say Holland just emotes and protects himself and loses a decision.

Pitbull vs. Pico
Aaron Pico, TKO, R1
This is hilarious matchmaking but also really a good fight. I think Pico wins through his youth. He was actually looking damn good imo against Murphy. He just needs to work on not being so open to getting hit, but even then Pitbull doesn’t have one shot knockout power at this stage of his career. I think Pico goes out and bullies him because he doesn’t have any fear of what’s coming back at him.

Gamrot vs. Ribovics
Mateusz Gamrot, DEC
Gamrot gets him. Decision. Basically exactly what he did to Ludovit Klein. The gate will be kept. I honestly think that using volume striking to try and stop grapplers is not the move. Ribovics tries to land with volume, throws 250 strikes a fight, does not get tired. Gamrot also doesn’t get tired, and will be able to keep Ribovics down on the ground imo.

Gastelum vs. Luque
Kelvin Gastelum, DEC
Washed vs mega washed. But you get to pick which one is which. Flip a coin, that’s the winner.

Mederos vs. Padilla
Chris Padilla, TKO, R2
Padilla looked damn good against Bonfim. If yall want to watch someone actually quit in the cage watch Padilla vs Bonfim. Padilla wins this with his superior durability and cardio.
